'cause they've got no income coming in from racing

Oh, you go. No, you go Steve,
It, it's a quick stop on the finances of it
because there has been a suggestion

income coming from the Australian gambling, that
that should go to Greyhound racing New Zealand

Yeah, well, uh, effectively that's the income from

and they, they determine the split within, you know,
Greyhound racing gets a share of that.

then potentially there's, there's another 40 to $50 million,
uh, you know, that someone's gotta pay for it.
Uh, and there's nothing in the bill

Uh, the income we get from the imported racing, uh,
from Australia is, is roughly eight to 10 million a year.
So there's a massive deficit
that no one's quite comprehending quite yet.
Uh, and even if we did continue racing, uh,
our market share would decline as the volume

our income will decline,
but we've still got dogs to feed, dogs to be home, uh,
and participant compensation to consider.
Okay. I have a start going back to the previous line

you talked about extending it

how the investment back into the track would be, would,
would wane as time went on when we knew it was gonna
be a finite industry.
So how would you ensure that that didn't then increase?
How would you give reassurance to those people

How would you give them reassurance

Industry? Yeah. Well, again, it depends
on
where the income comes from.
So currently we spend eight
to 10 million a year on welfare initiatives.
Um, track safety initiatives development.

that investment can continue.
Uh, but there will come a tipping point
where if the income drops off,
the money's gotta come from somewhere else.
So someone has to pay for it.
Uh, and, and that's, that's the choice.
You know, if the money's not there,
you can't spend the money on either feeding the dogs

in a safe racing ecosystem.
Thank you very much. Miles, you've still got your hand up?
Yeah, I've got another question
Actually.
Okay. Make it quick.

Okay. With last one.
